_comment: Per-agent and per-model behaviour for the task ledger. Copy to <project>/.agent/ledger/profiles.json.
Keys here override the built-in defaults in ledger.py; anything you omit falls back to the built-in.
This file is the evolution point: a new agent or a new model tier is an edit here, not a code change.
view          'full' renders the whole ledger; 'compact' renders only open work plus a settled roll-up.
enforce       whether the Stop gate blocks for this profile.
brief_includes  which sections a sub-agent brief carries. Ordered by how much context the model can
afford: 'verbatim' (the user's own words) and 'acceptance' are the two that should
survive every budget; 'detail' and 'siblings' are what you drop first.

claude-code.note: Large context: the whole ledger fits, and sub-agent briefs can afford sibling context.

codex.note: Codex Stop hooks use the {continue,stopReason,systemMessage} schema, not {decision}. See scripts/codex_task_ledger.sh.

opencode.note: Enforced through the opencode plugin API rather than a shell hook.

small-context.note: For models where the full ledger would crowd out the work. Open tasks only; briefs keep the user's words and the acceptance bar and drop everything else.
